Question: How did Paul become an apostle?
Response: Paul became an apostle through Jesus Christ and God the Father.

Question: From what have believers in Jesus Christ been delivered?
Response: Believers in Jesus Christ have been delivered from this present evil age.

Question: With what is Paul amazed with the church in Galatia?
Response: Paul is amazed that they are turning so quickly to a different gospel.

Question: How many true gospels are there?
Response: There is only one true gospel, the gospel of Christ.

Question: What does Paul say should happen to anyone proclaiming a different gospel than the gospel of Christ?
Response: Paul says anyone proclaiming a different gospel should be cursed.

Question: Servants of Christ must seek the approval of whom first?
Response: Servants of Christ must seek the approval of God first.

Question: How did Paul receive the knowledge of the gospel of Christ?
Response: Paul received the gospel of Christ by revelation from Jesus Christ directly to himself.

Question: What was Paul doing in his life before he received the revelation of the gospel of Christ?
Response: Paul was following Judaism zealously, persecuting the church of God and destroying it.

Question: When did God choose Paul to be his apostle?
Response: God was pleased to choose Paul from his mother’s womb to be his apostle.

Question: For what purpose did God choose Paul as his apostle?
Response: God chose Paul as his apostle so that Paul would proclaim Christ among the Gentiles.

Question: Where did Paul finally meet some of the other apostles?
Response: Finally, Paul went to Jerusalem and met the apostles Cephas and James.

Question: What were the churches in Judea hearing about Paul?
Response: The churches in Judea were hearing that Paul, who once persecuted the church, was now proclaiming the faith.

Question: What did Paul do when he went up to Jerusalem after 14 years?
Response: Paul spoke privately to the leaders of the church, explaining to them the gospel that he was proclaiming.

Question: What was Titus, a Gentile, not required to do?
Response: Titus was not required to be circumcised.

Question: What did the false brothers desire to do?
Response: The false brothers desired to make Paul and his companions slaves to the law.

Question: Did the leaders of the church in Jerusalem change Paul’s message?
Response: No, they added nothing to Paul’s message.

Question: To whom was Paul primarily sent to proclaim the gospel?
Response: Paul was primarily sent to the uncircumcised, the Gentiles, to proclaim the gospel.

Question: To whom was Peter primarily sent to proclaim the gospel?
Response: Peter was primarily sent to the circumcised, the Jews, to proclaim the gospel.

Question: How did the leaders in Jerusalem show their approval of Paul’s ministry?
Response: The leaders in Jerusalem gave Paul and Barnabas the right hand of fellowship to show their approval.

Question: What error did Peter make when he came to Antioch?
Response: Peter stopped eating with the Gentiles, because he feared the men who had been circumcised.

Question: What did Paul ask Cephas in front of everyone?
Response: Paul asked Cephas how he could force Gentiles to live like Jews when Cephas was living like a Gentile.

Question: Paul said that no one is justified by what?
Response: Paul said that no one is justified by the works of the law.

Question: How is a person justified before God?
Response: A person is justified before God by faith in Christ Jesus.

Question: If someone returns to trying to follow the law after having faith in Christ, what does Paul say he has actually become?
Response: Paul says he shows himself actually to be a law breaker.

Question: Who did Paul say now lived in him?
Response: Paul said that Christ now lived in him.

Question: What does Paul say the Son of God did for him?
Response: Paul says the Son of God loved him and gave himself for Paul.

Question: How was Abraham considered righteous before God?
Response: Abraham believed God, and it was credited to him as righteousness.

Question: Who are the sons of Abraham?
Response: Those who believe God are the sons of Abraham.

Question: The scripture foresaw that the Gentiles would be justified in what way?
Response: The scripture foresaw that the Gentiles would be justified by faith.

Question: Those who rely on the works of the law to be justified are under what?
Response: Those who rely on the works of the law to be justified are under a curse.

Question: How many people have been justified by God through the works of the law?
Response: No one has been justified through the works of the law.

Question: Why did Christ redeem us by becoming a curse for us?
Response: Christ redeemed us by becoming a curse for us so that the blessing upon Abraham might come to the Gentiles.

Question: Who was the “descendant” that was spoken of in the promise to Abraham?
Response: The “descendant” spoken of in the promise to Abraham was Christ.

Question: Did the coming of the Jewish law 430 years after Abraham void the promise God made to Abraham?
Response: No, the law did not void the promise made to Abraham.

Question: Why then was there the law?
Response: The law came because of transgressions until Abraham’s descendant came.

Question: What did the law in scripture imprison everyone under?
Response: The law in scripture imprisoned everyone under sin.

Question: How are we released from the imprisonment of the law?
Response: We are released from the imprisonment of the law by faith in Christ Jesus.

Question: Who has been clothed in Christ?
Response: All who have been baptized into Christ have been clothed in Christ.

Question: What different kinds of persons are made one in Christ Jesus?
Response: Jews, Greeks, slaves, free, male, and female are all made one in Christ Jesus.

Question: How does the heir of an estate live while he is a child?
Response: The heir lives like a slave under guardians and trustees until the time set by his father.

Question: What did God do at the right time in history?
Response: At the right time, God sent forth his Son to redeem those under the law.

Question: How did God bring children who were under the law into his family?
Response: God adopted as sons the children who were under the law.

Question: What does God send forth into the hearts of his children?
Response: God sends forth the Spirit of his Son into the hearts of his children.

Question: Before we know God, to whom are we slaves?
Response: Before we know God, we are slaves to the spirits that rule the world, who are not gods at all.

Question: Paul was perplexed that the Galatians were returning to what?
Response: Paul was perplexed that the Galatians were returning again to the ruling spirits of the world.

Question: As he sees the Galatians turning back, what does Paul fear for them?
Response: Paul fears that the Galatians will become slaves again, and that he has labored in vain over them.

Question: When Paul first came to the Galatians, what problem did he have?
Response: When Paul first came to the Galatians, he had a physical illness.

Question: Despite Paul’s problem, how did the Galatians receive him?
Response: Despite Paul’s problem, the Galatians received Paul as an angel of God, as Christ Jesus.

Question: Who are the false teachers in Galatia trying to separate?
Response: The false teachers are trying to separate the Galatians from Paul.

Question: Under what are the false teachers trying to put the Galatians?
Response: The false teachers are trying to put the Galatians back under the law.

Question: From which two kinds of women did Abraham have two sons?
Response: Abraham had two sons, one from a slave woman and one from a free woman.

Question: Who is the symbolic mother of Paul and the believing Galatians?
Response: The Jerusalem above, the free woman, is the symbolic mother of Paul and the believing Galatians.

Question: Are believers in Christ children of the flesh or children of the promise?
Response: Believers in Christ are children of the promise.

Question: Who persecutes the children of the promise?
Response: The children of the flesh persecute the children of the promise.

Question: With whom do the children of the slave woman not inherit?
Response: The children of the slave woman do not inherit along with the children of the free woman.

Question: Are believers in Christ children of the slave woman or children of the free woman?
Response: Believers in Christ are children of the free woman.

Question: For what purpose has Christ set us free?
Response: For freedom Christ has set us free.

Question: What did Paul warn the Galatians would happen if they became circumcised?
Response: Paul said that if the Galatians became circumcised, Christ would not benefit them in any way.

Question: What did Paul warn would happen to all the Galatians who would seek to be justified by following the law?
Response: Paul warned that all the Galatians who would seek to be justified by following the law would be alienated from Christ and would fall away from grace.

Question: As opposed to circumcision and uncircumcised, what is the only thing that means anything in Christ Jesus?
Response: In Christ Jesus, only faith working through love means anything.

Question: Of what is Paul confident regarding the one who has confused the Galatians about the gospel?
Response: Paul is confident that the one who has confused the Galatians about the gospel will bear God’s judgment.

Question: Paul says that proclaiming circumcision does what?
Response: Paul says that in proclaiming circumcision, the stumbling block of the cross would be destroyed.

Question: How are believers not to use their freedom in Christ?
Response: Believers are to not use their freedom in Christ as an opportunity for the flesh.

Question: How are believers to use their freedom in Christ?
Response: Believers are to use their freedom in Christ to serve one another in love.

Question: The whole law is fulfilled in what one commandment?
Response: The whole law is fulfilled in the commandment, “You must love your neighbor as yourself.”

Question: How can believers not fulfill the lust of the flesh?
Response: Believers can live by the Spirit, and thus, not fulfill the lust of the flesh.

Question: What two things are opposed to each other within the believer?
Response: The Spirit and the flesh are opposed to each other within the believer.

Question: What are three examples of works of the flesh?
Response: Three examples of works of the flesh are any three of the following list: sexual immorality, impurity, lustfulness, idolatry, sorcery, hostility, strife, jealousy, outbursts of anger, rivalry, dissension, sectarian division, envy, drunkenness, and drunken riots.

Question: What will those who practice the works of the flesh not receive?
Response: Those who practice the works of the flesh will not inherit the kingdom of God.

Question: What is the fruit of the Spirit?
Response: The fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, patience, kindness, goodness, faith, gentleness, and self-control.

Question: Those who belong to Christ Jesus have done what with the flesh and its passions?
Response: Those who belong to Christ Jesus have crucified the flesh and its passions.

Question: What should those who are spiritual do if a man is caught in some transgression?
Response: Those who are spiritual should restore that man in a spirit of gentleness.

Question: For what danger must those who are spiritual watch out?
Response: Those who are spiritual must watch out that they are not also tempted.

Question: How do believers fulfill the law of Christ?
Response: Believers fulfill the law of Christ by carrying one another’s burdens.

Question: How can a person have something in himself to be proud of regarding his work?
Response: A person can have something in himself to be proud of by examining his own work, without comparing himself to anyone else.

Question: What must one who is taught the word do with his teacher?
Response: One who is taught the word must share all good things with his teacher.

Question: What happens to whatever a man spiritually plants?
Response: Whatever a man spiritually plants he will harvest.

Question: What does a man harvest who plants to his own flesh?
Response: A man who plants to his own flesh harvests destruction out of his flesh.

Question: What does a man harvest who plants to the Spirit?
Response: Out of the Spirit a man who plants to the Spirit harvests eternal life.

Question: If a believer does not give up and continues doing good, what will he receive?
Response: A believer who continues doing good will reap a harvest.

Question: To whom should believers especially do good?
Response: Believers should especially do good to those of the household of faith.

Question: What is the motivation of those who want to compel the believers to be circumcised?
Response: Those who want to compel the believers to be circumcised do not want to be persecuted for the cross of Christ.

Question: Of what did Paul say he was proud?
Response: Paul said that he was proud of the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ.

Question: Instead of circumcision or uncircumcision, what is important?
Response: What is important is a new creation.

Question: Upon whom does Paul wish peace and mercy?
Response: Paul wishes peace and mercy upon those who live by the rule of the new creation and upon the Israel of God.

Question: What did Paul carry on his body?
Response: Paul carried the marks of Jesus on his body.
